author: Ali Muhammad, Maulvi
title: Bara Imam
gloss: A booklet giving short biographical sketches of the lives of twelve Imams, viz., Ali and his decendants.

author: Ali Muhammad, Maulvi
title: Das Jannati
gloss: Short biographical sketches of the lives of the ten disciples of Muhammad—the first four caliphs, and Talha Zubair, Abdur Rahman, Sa'd, Said and A'bu Abaida—

author: Muhammad Din, Fauq
title: Abul Hasan Mulla Do piaza Ki Sawanih Umri
gloss: A brief sketch of the life of Abul Hasan, alias Mullah Do Piaza, one of the courtiers of Akbar, the Mughal Emperor.

printer_verbatim: Newal Kishore Gas Printing Works, Lahore.
title: Sher-i-Punjab yani Maharaja Ranjit Singh Ji
gloss: The Lion of the Punjab. A biographical sketch of Maharaja Ranjit Singh, with an account of the rise and fall of the Sikh Empire. Translated into Urdu prose, by Ghulam Ali Khan, from the original in Persian poetry by late Rai Kanhaya Lal, Hindi.
publisher: Sardar Jot Singh Sant Singh, Booksellers

author: Habib Ullah, Asi
title: Taqdir ki Gardish almaruf Sat Harish Chandra
gloss: A drama of Harish Chandra who, according to a promise gave his kingdom to a devotee and was reduced to utter penury.

printer_verbatim: Civil and Military Organ Press, Ludhiana.
author: Jullundhri Ram, Lala
title: Mukammal Drama Muhsin Kush urf Fateh Jang
gloss: Malik Adil, a king of Persia, raised Fateh Jang, a man of low status, to the rank of a Nawab and appointed him the ruler of Isphahan. The Nawab rebelled and defeated the king in a battle. The king committed suicide but his son eventually defeated Fateh Jang.
publisher: Shaikh Allah Bakhsh Ilahi Bakhsh, Booksellers

flags.issue: serial number partly obscured, read as 5

author: Jullundhri Ram, Lala
title: Mukammal Drama Jam-i-Jahan Numa urf Aina-i-Sikandri
gloss: A drama adapted to the Indian stage. A prince who already had two wives is loved by a fairy. The fairy takes the prince to her own abode. The wives of the prince and the minister discover and release the prince through the agency of "Jam-i-Jahan Numa," a magic cup.
publisher: Shaikh Allah Bakhsh Ilahi Bakhsh, Booksellers
